audit_items.proof_trace: get_compressed_input_stream and get_compressed_output_stream (compressed_stream.cc) return NULL in the default case for unknown compress_type values, guarded only by SCHECK(false) which calls abort() via the default log handler. If set_log_handler(NULL) is installed (an explicitly supported API), SCHECK becomes a no-op and NULL propagates: is.get() is passed unchecked to ParseFromZeroCopyStream (L92), and os->Flush() (L162 in AssembleSucceedResponse) directly dereferences the scoped_ptr with no guard. No null check exists after get_compressed_input/output_stream returns.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker sends a binary RPC request with an unrecognized compress_type integer in the wire format; if the server has called set_log_handler(NULL) to suppress logging, get_compressed_input_stream returns NULL and request->ParseFromZeroCopyStream(NULL) or os->Flush() (scoped_ptr deref) crashes the server.
